findings.description: Page 2 of the PDF contains two distinct sections: the main quote form (repeated/duplicated content) and additional fragments. The HTML conversion renders this as a multi-column table layout that collapses these into a different structure.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows the full RFQ form repeated with overlapping fragments. HTML page 2 renders this as a structured three-column table format with Column 1, Column 2, Column 3 headers, which is a different structural interpretation.

findings.description: Authorized signature images/handwritten signatures present in the PDF are missing from the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: PDF shows handwritten signatures for Brian J. McCrodden and the DENR authorized representative. HTML does not display these signature images.

findings.description: Signature images are missing from HTML on page 1.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows handwritten authorized signature and DENR acceptance signature. HTML does not display these.

findings.description: The vendor information form on page 1 is rendered differently. PDF shows it as a structured form with fields in a grid-like layout, while HTML renders it as labeled text pairs.
findings.evidence: PDF has a visual form layout with boxes for VENDOR, STREET ADDRESS, CITY & STATE & ZIP, etc. alongside FEDERAL ID, P.O. BOX, TELEPHONE NUMBER fields. HTML converts this to a simpler text format.

findings.description: The item cost table on page 3 - Item 4 cost column is missing the dollar amount alignment but data is present.
findings.evidence: PDF shows all 4 items with costs aligned in the COST column. HTML preserves the same data and structure.

findings.description: Page 4 of the PDF shows vendor information form at top followed by the award criteria and furnish/deliver sections. HTML page 4 restructures this as a three-column comparison table.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 has the form fields in standard layout. HTML renders Column 1, Column 2, Column 3 as a comparative table structure.

findings.description: Pages 5-8 of the PDF contain ITS Terms and Conditions. The HTML conversion appears to duplicate some of this content, showing both scanned image text and re-rendered text versions.
findings.evidence: PDF pages show single instances of ITS Terms sections. HTML shows fragments from scanned pages alongside fully rendered text versions of the same content.

findings.description: The reading order on pages with duplicated/overlapping content from the PDF scan is not always preserved correctly in HTML, with fragments appearing in different sequences.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 shows continuous Terms and Conditions text. HTML page 6 shows fragmented text blocks from the scan mixed with re-rendered versions.

findings.description: Moffatt & Nichol company logo/letterhead on Exhibit 2 page is not visible in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page showing Exhibit 2 has a company letterhead/logo at top. HTML does not show this image.

findings.description: Pages 3-6 of the PDF contain extensive Instructions to Vendors (Items 1-13) and other content that precedes the ITS Terms and Conditions section. This content is entirely missing from the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF has content on pages that include numbered items from the Instructions to Vendors section (items 1-13 at minimum) before the ITS Terms and Conditions. The HTML jumps from page 2 content directly to a fragment about 'documentation for the protest' from page 3, omitting the Instructions to Vendors section entirely.

findings.description: Exhibit 2 (Moffatt & Nichol scope of work for Deliverable 2) pages 8-9 are partially missing. The HTML omits the beginning of Exhibit 2, including the header, scope description of agricultural demand data methodology, and multiple sections.
findings.evidence: PDF page 8 (after Exhibit 1 page 8) contains Exhibit 2 with detailed scope including '1.0 Scope of Work' sections about agricultural demand data estimation methods. The HTML only picks up partway through with 'For those crops typically irrigated...' missing the exhibit header and initial scope sections.

findings.description: The vendor information table on page 1 is restructured with fabricated column headers ('Column 1', 'Column 2', 'Column 3') and duplicated data in some cells.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows a structured form layout. The HTML creates a 3-column table with generic 'Column 1/2/3' headers. The 'FEDERAL ID OR SOCIAL SECURITY NO. 52-1413880' appears duplicated in columns 2 and 3. Similarly 'FAX NUMBER: 919-831-1295' is duplicated across columns 2 and 3.

findings.description: The page header table also uses fabricated generic column headers 'Column 1' and 'Column 2' instead of meaningful headers.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a structured form with State/Department info on left and RFQ details on right. HTML uses generic 'Column 1' / 'Column 2' headers.

findings.description: The 'Where will services be performed' row is duplicated across all three columns showing the same content, when in the PDF it appears as a single-line entry.
findings.evidence: HTML shows 'Where will services be performed: ______________________' repeated in three separate cells for one row.

findings.description: The collusion statute reference is incorrect. The PDF states 'G.S. 147-33-100' but this is faithfully reproduced - however 'Moffatt & Nichols' in the PDF page 2 is rendered as 'Moffatt & Nichols' in one place but 'Moffatt & Nichol' in others, matching the inconsistency in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2: 'Moffatt & Nichols will develop historical municipal...' - HTML preserves this. Exhibit 2 uses 'Moffatt & Nichol' (without s) which is also preserved.

findings.description: The Exhibit 2 cost total in the PDF shows '$96,200' but the HTML Item 3 in the main bid table shows '$99,000' - however both match the PDF correctly as these are different figures (Moffatt & Nichol subcontract total vs. HydroLogics bid line item).
findings.evidence: Both figures appear correctly in PDF and HTML - this is not an error.

findings.description: No h1 heading exists in the document. Multiple h3 headings are used at the same level without a clear hierarchy. The document title 'REQUEST FOR QUOTES NO. 261' should be the h1.
findings.evidence: All section headings use h3: 'NOTICE TO VENDOR', 'EXECUTION', 'ACCEPTANCE OF QUOTE', 'ITS Terms and Conditions', 'Exhibit 1', etc. No h1 or h2 headings are used.

findings.description: The contract acceptance date in the HTML says 'day of JANUARY, 2008' but the PDF shows handwritten text that appears to read 'JANUARY' with '2008' partially visible - this is a reasonable interpretation of the handwriting.
findings.evidence: PDF 'FOR DENR USE ONLY' section has handwritten date. HTML renders as '1 day of JANUARY, 2008'.

findings.description: The vendor email in the PDF reads 'bmccrodden@hydrologics.net' but the HTML renders it as 'bmccrodden@hydrologics.net' - matching correctly.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show the same email address.
